title: Managing Kubernetes Objects Using Imperative Commands — Using--editto modify objects before creation
text:
You can use
kubectl create --edit
to make arbitrary changes to an object
before it is created. Here's an example:
kubectl create service clusterip my-svc --clusterip
=
"None"
-o yaml --dry-run
=
client > /tmp/srv.yaml
kubectl create --edit -f /tmp/srv.yaml
The
kubectl create service
command creates the configuration for the Service and saves it to
/tmp/srv.yaml
.
The
kubectl create --edit
command opens the configuration file for editing before it creates the object.
